title: gaultheria leucocarpa f. cumingiana
intro: Gaultheria leucocarpa f. cumingiana, also known as the White Mountain Cranberry, is a plant species belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections and reduce pain. The health benefits of Gaultheria leucocarpa f. cumingiana include its ability to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat various ailments, such as urinary tract infections, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
